headline: A new radical left is shaking up the Democratic party
subtitle: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
subtitle: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

headline: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France, a sudden move with far-reaching consequences
subtitle: Captain Ibrahim Traoré announced on June 26 that Burkina Faso will break off bilateral relations with Paris. The 2,500 French nationals living in the country will no longer be able to rely on embassy or consular services, as staff are being repatriated.

headline: Heat and acoustics: The challenges of outdoor opera at Aix-en-Provence
subtitle: Thunderstorms, wind, sun, lack of reverberation: Confronted with the limitations of the Théâtre de l'Archevêché, the Festival d'Aix-en-Provence opera festival has turned to technology to make the experience more comfortable for both the audience and the musicians.

headline: How can we prepare together for the next heatwave?
subtitle: France will experience many more heatwaves and some will be even more severe than the last one, which was historic. To cope, we need to get organized collectively – without giving up the fight to reduce our greenhouse gas emissions, Le Monde's climate expert writes.

subtitle: Vadym Yermolaiev, a wealthy businessman, was targeted by a homemade explosive bomb on Monday. Despite renouncing his Ukrainian nationality, he continued business activities connected to his country of origin.

headline: 250 years of American independence: In 1976, a bicentennial celebrated with patriotism despite divisions
subtitle: 'America 250' (10/13). In 1976, the United States turned a page on one of the most turbulent chapters in its history. After the Vietnam War and the resignation of Richard Nixon, the bicentennial offered Americans a moment of relief. Minority rights, social justice – new debates continued to stir society, notably led by the economist Jeremy Rifkin and the 'red, white and blue' left.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subtitle: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
